channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Soft robotic prosthetic hand uses nerve signals for more natural control
item.description: Researchers unveiled a new soft prosthetic hand that combines the natural coordination patterns of our fingers with the decoding of motoneuron activity in the spinal column.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch Brit Awards 2025 online from anywhere and for free
item.description: With Jack Whitehall hosting and Sabrina Carpenter, The Last Dinner Party and Sam Fender performing, here’s how you can watch Brit Awards 2025 from anywhere.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is this the perfect portable router? GL-iNet's latest model offers dual SIM, load balancing, gigabit LAN ports and four massive antennas
item.description: GL.iNet’s Spitz Plus GL-X2000 merges dual SIM speeds with bandwidth bonding, offering a fast, stable Wi-Fi 6 connection for mobile users and remote locations.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alexa+ – Here’s how to sign up for early access
item.description: Amazon's now letting customers in the United States sign up for early access to Alexa+. Here's how to do just that and the Echo Show device you'll need to be eligible.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Government Agencies' AI Strategies: Progress and Best Practices
item.description: Government agencies are leveraging artificial intelligence technology to achieve their missions. Top experts share strategies, lessons learned, challenges, and solutions in this webinar.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011 and which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas
item.description: Federal use cases typically fall into one of five “swim lanes,” with most impacting customer or citizen services, said the IDC Insight's federal researcher. The post AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Skype to shut 14 years after Microsoft’s $8.5 billion purchase
item.description: Once a byword for digital calls that bypassed long-distance charges, Skype was surpassed in recent years by smartphone-native communication apps and Zoom video calls.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Everything new on Netflix in March 2025 – stream the miniseries Adolescence and new Original movies
item.description: Netflix's March 2025 list of new movies and shows is a reflection of why it's still one of the best streaming services. Here's everything new this month, including original shows and old favorites.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: F5 targets AI application security with renewed workload management software
item.description: F5 is evolving its core application and load balancing software to help customers secure and manage AI-powered and multicloud workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift improves virtualization support
item.description: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18, the latest version of the company’s Kubernetes-powered application platform, has arrived, with enhancements designed to simplify management of virtual machines and containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: I’m a tech expert. These are the 5 time-saving tech tips I tell everyone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson presents five secret shortcuts: Adjust sound/vibration, share YouTube moments, fix tabs and tweak brightness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
